generatedSentence: In the opulent, yet abandoned city, a gray usurper had risen. The nonesuch prophets spoke of tectonics and how pantheons would disappear briskly at noon. We could only suppose he was outmatched, obviously relying on a rigged system. He was equipped with invisible technology, making any extraction an unreal heist. An actress, once a lamplighter, would reopen old fiction to uncover the truth. Our leadership grumped weekly, debating whether to omit the mention of fracking that would pervade the city. They generalize the uneasy truth, seeing it as a boomerang of actions from Ottoman perfidy. We had to rethink the disjointed intelligence, especially with occasional gunfights near the heliports. The inflation was huge, but they refused to commit more joules to ensure our headway, fearing a goldrush. The climax arrived at noon, a desperate heist in the french quarter. Our team, with brains atrophying from the dryness, had to lighten the freight of embargoed lichees. Carrying kilos of the premium fruit, they became totally outmatched in gunfights, feeling the needles of doubt tightly pervade. One could only mention the hateful pictograph on their ford, a symbol of their decline, humbles as a koi. In the daytime, the resolution was bittersweet. The nanobot technology, once vibrating in his eardrum, was extracted. We would unload the data, a sampler of their perfidy, to insure newborns against future usurpers. The utopian vision, once a jockstrap on our hopes, could now genuinely flourish, like vetch and trees in the sun. This endowment, a gift from our faraday, would lighten the future, removing all ambiguity.
panelSentences: In the opulent, yet abandoned city, a gray usurper had risen. The nonesuch prophets spoke of tectonics and how pantheons would disappear briskly at noon. We could only suppose he was outmatched, obviously relying on a rigged system. He was equipped with invisible technology, making any extraction an unreal heist. An actress, once a lamplighter, would reopen old fiction to uncover the truth.

Our leadership grumped weekly, debating whether to omit the mention of fracking that would pervade the city. They generalize the uneasy truth, seeing it as a boomerang of actions from Ottoman perfidy. We had to rethink the disjointed intelligence, especially with occasional gunfights near the heliports. The inflation was huge, but they refused to commit more joules to ensure our headway, fearing a goldrush.

The climax arrived at noon, a desperate heist in the french quarter. Our team, with brains atrophying from the dryness, had to lighten the freight of embargoed lichees. Carrying kilos of the premium fruit, they became totally outmatched in gunfights, feeling the needles of doubt tightly pervade. One could only mention the hateful pictograph on their ford, a symbol of their decline, humbles as a koi.

In the daytime, the resolution was bittersweet. The nanobot technology, once vibrating in his eardrum, was extracted. We would unload the data, a sampler of their perfidy, to insure newborns against future usurpers. The utopian vision, once a jockstrap on our hopes, could now genuinely flourish, like vetch and trees in the sun. This endowment, a gift from our faraday, would lighten the future, removing all ambiguity.
rawPanelSentences: In the opulent, yet abandoned city, a gray usurper had risen. The nonesuch prophets spoke of tectonics and how pantheons would disappear briskly at noon. We could only suppose he was outmatched, obviously relying on a rigged system. He was equipped with invisible technology, making any extraction an unreal heist. An actress, once a lamplighter, would reopen old fiction to uncover the truth.

Our leadership grumped weekly, debating whether to omit the mention of fracking that would pervade the city. They generalize the uneasy truth, seeing it as a boomerang of actions from Ottoman perfidy. We had to rethink the disjointed intelligence, especially with occasional gunfights near the heliports. The inflation was huge, but they refused to commit more joules to ensure our headway, fearing a goldrush.

The climax arrived at noon, a desperate heist in the french quarter. Our team, with brains atrophying from the dryness, had to lighten the freight of embargoed lichees. Carrying kilos of the premium fruit, they became totally outmatched in gunfights, feeling the needles of doubt tightly pervade. One could only mention the hateful pictograph on their ford, a symbol of their decline, humbles as a koi.

In the daytime, the resolution was bittersweet. The nanobot technology, once vibrating in his eardrum, was extracted. We would unload the data, a sampler of their perfidy, to insure newborns against future usurpers. The utopian vision, once a jockstrap on our hopes, could now genuinely flourish, like vetch and trees in the sun. This endowment, a gift from our faraday, would lighten the future, removing all ambiguity.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone, masked figure, wearing a deep indigo trench coat and glowing crimson cybernetic glasses. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The masked figure stands atop a crumbling, abandoned skyscraper, observing a sprawling, neon-lit cityscape below, viewed from a high-angle long shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The masked figure, now in a tense, crouched position, narrowly dodges laser fire from an unseen enemy, dynamic low-angle mid-shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The masked figure, silhouetted against a brilliant explosion in the distance, makes a heroic leap across a chasm between two buildings, wide-angle action shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The masked figure stands triumphant, gazing at a newly established, illuminated utopian garden, with a subtle smirk visible through their glasses, medium shot from a slightly elevated angle.

To the fullest extent or degree.
definitions.example: They've totally demolished the bank on Main Street.

definitions.definition: (degree) Very; extremely.
definitions.example: That was totally wicked!

definitions.definition: (modal) Definitely; for sure.
definitions.example: That was totally not what happened.

definitions.definition: To fit out with a harness or other equipment.
To equip and fit (a ship) with sails, shrouds, and yards.
To dress or clothe in some costume.
To make or construct something in haste or in a makeshift manner.

definitions.definition: To manipulate something dishonestly for personal gain or discriminatory purposes.
definitions.example: to rig an election

definitions.definition: To make free with; hence, to steal; to pilfer.
To outfit a model with controls for animation.
To play the wanton; to act in an unbecoming manner; to play tricks.
(for a trial, election, or competition) Pre-arranged and fixed so that the winner or outcome is decided in advance.
definitions.example: There was a vicious rumour that the final was rigged, as the defense seemed useless.

definitions.definition: (typically not comparable) Having the rigging up.
definitions.example: We were ready to embark upon our journey now the vessel was rigged.

definitions.definition: An upholstered sofa, without arms or a back, sometimes with a compartment for storing linen, etc.
A low stool or thick cushion used to rest the feet or as a seat.
A fabric with a pronounced ribbed or corded effect, often made of silk or a mixture of cotton and silk-like yarns.
The Chinese tropical fruit tree Litchi chinensis, of the soapberry family.
That tree's bright red oval fruit with a single stone surrounded by a fleshy white aril.
A soft pink-red colour, like that of a lychee rind (also called lychee red).

A location where a stream is shallow and the bottom has good footing, making it possible to cross from one side to the other with no bridge, by walking, riding, or driving through the water; a crossing.
A stream; a current.
To cross a stream using a ford.

In the International System of Units, the base unit of mass; conceived of as the mass of one litre of water, but now defined by taking the fixed numerical value of the Planck constant h to be 6.626 070 15 × 10-34 when expressed in units of kg⋅m2⋅s−1. Symbol: kg
The unit of weight such that a one-kilogram mass is also a one-kilogram weight.
An athletic supporter worn by men to support the genitals during strenuous exercise.
Something done so as to accomplish a purpose.
A way of motion or functioning.
definitions.example: Knead bread with a rocking action.

definitions.definition: Fast-paced activity.
definitions.example: a movie full of exciting action

definitions.definition: A mechanism; a moving part or assembly.
definitions.example: a rifle action

definitions.definition: The mechanism, that is the set of moving mechanical parts, of a keyboard instrument, like a piano, which transfers the motion of the key to the sound-making device.
The distance separating the strings and the fretboard on a guitar.
Sexual intercourse.
definitions.example: She gave him some action.

definitions.definition: Combat.
definitions.example: He saw some action in the Korean War.

definitions.definition: A charge or other process in a law court (also called lawsuit and actio).

A mapping from a pairing of mathematical objects to one of them, respecting their individual structures. The pairing is typically a Cartesian product or a tensor product. The object that is not part of the output is said to act on the other object. In any given context, action is used as an abbreviation for a more fully named notion, like group action or left group action.
The product of energy and time, especially the product of the Lagrangian and time.
The event or connected series of events, either real or imaginary, forming the subject of a play, poem, or other composition; the unfolding of the drama of events.
(painting and sculpture) The attitude or position of the several parts of the body as expressive of the sentiment or passion depicted.
Spin put on the bowling ball.
A share in the capital stock of a joint-stock company, or in the public funds.
To act on a request etc, in order to put it into effect.
To initiate a legal action against someone.
Something with which a person or thing is endowed.
Property or funds invested for the support and benefit of a person or not-for-profit institution.
Endowment assurance or pure endowment.
A ceremony designed to prepare participants for their role in the afterlife.
Fake; not real
Very impressive; amazing; unbelievable; incredible; larger or more fantastic than typical of real life.
definitions.example: I just had an unreal hamburger.

definitions.definition: Fast, quickly, swiftly.
To make a pledge to (someone); to promise, guarantee (someone of something); to assure.
To make sure or certain of something (usually some future event or condition).
definitions.example: I use an alarm clock to ensure that I get up on time.

definitions.definition: To provide for compensation if some specified risk occurs. Often agreed by policy (contract) to offer financial compensation in case of an accident, theft or other undesirable event.
definitions.example: I'm not insured against burglary.

definitions.definition: To deal in such contracts; subscribe to a policy of insurance
A major appendage of human or animal, used for locomotion (such as an arm, leg or wing).
A branch of a tree.
The part of the bow, from the handle to the tip.
An elementary piece of the mechanism of a lock.
A thing or person regarded as a part or member of, or attachment to, something else.
The part of a corolla beyond the throat.
To remove the limbs from (an animal or tree).
definitions.example: They limbed the felled trees before cutting them into logs.

definitions.definition: To supply with limbs.
The apparent visual edge of a celestial body.
definitions.example: solar limb

definitions.definition: (on a measuring instrument) The graduated edge of a circle or arc.
The border or upper spreading part of a monopetalous corolla, or of a petal or sepal; blade.
Movement ahead or forward.
Forward motion, or its rate.
The interval of time or distance between the fronts of two vehicles (e.g. buses) moving in succession in the same direction, especially along the same pre-determined route.
Progress toward a goal.
The clearance beneath an object, such as an arch, ceiling or bridge; headroom.
(coal-mining) A cross-heading.
A state or act of violating faith or allegiance; violation of a promise or vow, or of trust
Specifically, in warfare, an illegitimate act of deception, such as using symbols like the Red Cross or white flag to gain proximity to an enemy for purposes of attack.
A state or act of deceit.
To shake with small, rapid movements to and fro.
To resonate.
definitions.example: Her mind was vibrating with excitement.

definitions.definition: To brandish; to swing to and fro.
definitions.example: to vibrate a sword or a staff

definitions.definition: To mark or measure by moving to and fro.
definitions.example: a pendulum vibrating seconds

definitions.definition: To affect with vibratory motion; to set in vibration.
To please or impress someone.
To use vibrato.
A fine, sharp implement usually for piercing such as sewing, or knitting, acupuncture, tattooing, body piercing, medical injections, etc.
definitions.example: The seamstress threaded the needle to sew on a button.

definitions.definition: Any slender, pointed object resembling a needle, such as a pointed crystal, a sharp pinnacle of rock, an obelisk, etc.
A fine measurement indicator on a dial or graph, e.g. a compass needle.
definitions.example: The needle on the fuel gauge pointed to empty.

definitions.definition: A sensor for playing phonograph records, a phonograph stylus.
definitions.example: Ziggy bought some diamond needles for his hi-fi phonograph.

definitions.definition: A needle-like leaf found on some conifers.
A strong beam resting on props, used as a temporary support during building repairs.
(usually preceded by the) The death penalty carried out by lethal injection.
A text string that is searched for within another string. (see: needle in a haystack)
To pierce with a needle, especially for sewing or acupuncture.
To tease in order to provoke; to poke fun at.
definitions.example: Billy needled his sister incessantly about her pimples.

definitions.definition: To form, or be formed, in the shape of a needle.
definitions.example: to needle crystals

definitions.definition: In a tight manner.
definitions.example: tightly clenched fingers

definitions.definition: An order by the government prohibiting ships from leaving port.
A ban on trade with another country.
A temporary ban on making certain information public.
definitions.example: This copy of the federal budget is under embargo until 2 p.m.

definitions.definition: To impose an embargo on trading certain goods with another country.
To impose an embargo on a document.
A feeling of anger or annoyance caused by something offensive.
A feeling of doubt.
Leaves that provide shade, as the foliage of trees.
Shadow; shade.
To displease or cause offense.
To shade.
To make brighter or clearer; to illuminate.
definitions.example: to lighten an apartment with lamps or gas; to lighten the streets

definitions.definition: To become brighter or clearer; to brighten.
To burst forth or dart, as lightning; to shine with, or like, lightning; to flash.
To emit or disclose in, or as if in, lightning; to flash out, like lightning.
To illuminate with knowledge; to enlighten.
A person employed to light streetlights at dusk and snuff them at dawn.
(Baltimore) An arrest based on weak evidence intended to demean or punish the subject.
To defeat or reduce the power, independence, or pride of
To make humble or lowly; to make less proud or arrogant; to make meek and submissive.
A stag that has failed to grow antlers; a cow that has not developed horns.
Especially in hummel corn: grain that lacks awns (beards or bristles), or has had its awns removed (barley, oats, etc.).
Of an animal: to remove the horns; to poll.
To separate (barley, oats, etc.) from the awns.
Any of several leguminous plants, of the genus Vicia, often grown as green manure and for their edible seeds
Any of several similar plants within the subfamily Faboideae
A recently born baby.
The time of daylight; the time between sunrise and sunset.
Pertaining to daytime; appropriate to the day.
Happening during the day.
definitions.example: daytime television

definitions.definition: Someone who supports or heralds the establishment of a utopia.
Ideal but often impractical; visionary.
definitions.example: a utopian project

definitions.definition: Of or pertaining to or resembling a utopia.
definitions.example: utopian happiness

definitions.definition: A flat curved airfoil, that spins about an axis perpendicular to the direction of flight, that was originally used in various parts of the world as hunting weapons or, in returnable types, for sports or training.
A breakdancing move in which the performer walks on his or her hands while keeping the legs raised off the ground.
To return or rebound unexpectedly, especially when the result is undesired; to backfire.
To travel in a curved path.
The same.
Any distinctive identifying uniform worn by a group, such as the uniform worn by chauffeurs and male servants.
The whole body of liverymen, members of livery companies.
The paint scheme of a vehicle or fleet of vehicles.
definitions.example: The airline's new livery  received a mixed reaction from the press.

definitions.definition: A taxicab or limousine.
The delivery of property from one owner to the next.
The writ by which property is obtained.
The rental of horses or carriages; the rental of canoes; the care and/or boarding of horses for money.
A stable that keeps horses or carriages for rental.
An allowance of food; a ration, as given out to a family, to servants, to horses, etc.
Release from wardship; deliverance.
A low grade of wool.
Outward markings, fittings or appearance
To clothe.
definitions.example: He liveried his servants in the most modest of clothing.

definitions.definition: Ornamental domesticated varieties of the common carp (Cyprinus carpio), of Japan and eastern Asia with red-gold or white coloring.
Evoking a feeling of hatred.
Dislikeable.
Full of hatred.
Any period of feverish migration into an area in which gold has been discovered.
A cocktail made from bourbon, honey, and lemon juice.
The urge to win gold medals, as in the Olympic Games.
To be in every part of; to spread through.
definitions.example: Cruel wars pervade history.

definitions.definition: A very small autonomous robot, typically the size of a biological cell, designed to work alone or in very large numbers to achieve some task.
A wet nurse.
A person (usually a woman) who takes care of other people’s young.
definitions.example: They hired a nurse to care for their young boy.

definitions.definition: A person trained to provide care for the sick.
definitions.example: The nurse made her rounds through the hospital ward.

definitions.definition: One who, or that which, brings up, rears, causes to grow, trains, or fosters.
definitions.example: Eton College has been called "the chief nurse of England's statesmen".

definitions.definition: A shrub or tree that protects a young plant.
A lieutenant or first officer who takes command when the captain is unfit for his place.
A larva of certain trematodes, which produces cercariae by asexual reproduction.
A nurse shark.
To breastfeed: to feed (a baby) at the breast; to suckle.
definitions.example: She believes that nursing her baby will make him strong and healthy.

definitions.definition: To breastfeed: to be fed at the breast.
To care for (someone), especially in sickness; to tend to.
definitions.example: She nursed him back to health.

definitions.definition: To treat kindly and with extra care
definitions.example: She nursed the rosebush and that season it bloomed.

definitions.definition: To manage with care and economy
To drink slowly
To foster, to nourish
To hold closely to one's chest
definitions.example: Would you like to nurse the puppy?

definitions.definition: To strike (billiard balls) gently, so as to keep them in good position during a series of shots.
Fast growing function based on Kruskal's tree theorem.

A perennial woody plant, not exactly defined, but differentiated from a shrub by its larger size (typically over a few meters in height) or growth habit, usually having a single (or few) main axis or trunk unbranched for some distance above the ground and a head of branches and foliage.
definitions.example: Birds have a nest in a tree in the garden.

definitions.definition: Any plant that is reminiscent of the above but not classified as a tree (in any botanical sense).
definitions.example: the banana tree

definitions.definition: An object made from a tree trunk and having multiple hooks or storage platforms.
definitions.example: He had the choice of buying a scratching post or a cat tree.

definitions.definition: A device used to hold or stretch a shoe open.
definitions.example: He put a shoe tree in each of his shoes.

definitions.definition: The structural frame of a saddle.
A connected graph with no cycles or, if the graph is finite, equivalently a connected graph with n vertices and n−1 edges.
A recursive data structure in which each node has zero or more nodes as children.
A display or listing of entries or elements such that there are primary and secondary entries shown, usually linked by drawn lines or by indenting to the right.
definitions.example: We’ll show it as a tree list.

definitions.definition: Any structure or construct having branches representing divergence or possible choices.
definitions.example: family tree; skill tree

definitions.definition: The structure or wooden frame used in the construction of a saddle used in horse riding.
(in the plural) Marijuana.
A cross or gallows.

definitions.definition: Wood; timber.
A mass of crystals, aggregated in arborescent forms, obtained by precipitation of a metal from solution.
The fifth Lenormand card.
To chase (an animal or person) up a tree.
definitions.example: The dog treed the cat.

definitions.definition: To place in a tree.
definitions.example: Black bears can tree their cubs for protection, but grizzly bears cannot.

definitions.definition: To place upon a tree; to fit with a tree; to stretch upon a tree.
definitions.example: to tree a boot

definitions.definition: To take refuge in a tree.
The act of thinking again about something.

definitions.definition: To think again about a problem.
A thin membrane that separates the outer ear from the middle ear and transmits sound from the air to the malleus.
Payment for transportation.
definitions.example: The freight was more expensive for cars than for coal.

definitions.definition: Goods or items in transport.
definitions.example: The freight shifted and the trailer turned over on the highway.

definitions.definition: Transport of goods.
definitions.example: They shipped it ordinary freight to spare the expense.

definitions.definition: A freight train.
Cultural or emotional associations.
To transport (goods).
To load with freight. Also figurative.
The obligatory prayer that Muslims are called to perform five times a day.
A piece of needlework embroidered with a variety of designs.
A publication that is published once a week.
Of or relating to a week.
Happening once a week, or every week.
definitions.example: He's going for his weekly check-up at the hospital.

definitions.definition: Once every week.
definitions.example: She visits her mother weekly.

definitions.definition: Every week.
The position from which something is observed or considered; an angle, outlook or point of view.
Something, particularly words and sentences, that is open to more than one interpretation, explanation or meaning, if that meaning etc cannot be determined from its context.
definitions.example: His speech was made with such great ambiguity that neither supporter nor opponent could be certain of his true position.

definitions.definition: The state of being ambiguous.
A lack of moisture.
The degree to which something is dry.
Not easy; difficult.
The capacity of someone to lead others.
A group of leaders.
The office or status of a leader.
